thesis: Airbnb is the global short-term rental market's defining brand and leader. Q1 2026 revenue +17.9% beat expectations, providing the first hard data point supporting the growth re-acceleration narrative, but valuation has expanded from 29x to 35.4x P/E, pricing in much of this improvement. Margins and TTM FCF quality warrant monitoring.
catalysts: Q2 2026 earnings (expected August 2026) — can full-year growth momentum sustain?
2026 new product launch cadence (Experiences, AI features)
FIFA World Cup 2026 + Winter Olympics 2026 demand pulse
EU short-term rental regulations effective May 2026 — real-world impact assessment
New category (Experiences, boutique hotels) revenue contribution data
targetPrice: Analyst consensus ~$155-160 (~8-12% upside); Bull scenario P/E 30-35x implies ~$160-185

thesis: Amazon is a compounding machine powered by AWS, advertising, and retail flywheels, with AI investments reshaping its growth trajectory — but a $200B capex bet introduces unprecedented execution risk.
catalysts: AWS AI revenue acceleration (Q4 FY2025 +24% YoY, fastest in 13 quarters)
Prime Video ads and live sports monetization (NBA, NFL, UEFA)
targetPrice: No explicit price target stated; valuation dependent on $200B capex ROI and AWS growth trajectory

thesis: Bloom Energy is transitioning from a niche fuel-cell company into a critical AI-infrastructure power provider riding the structural grid-power deficit — but after a large 2026H1 re-rating, the stock has moved from 'high-growth infrastructure premium' to 'priced for near-perfect execution,' so orders, capacity, margins, and capital intensity must now be read on one page.
catalysts: Quarterly results testing the raised 2026 guidance ($3.1-3.3B)
targetPrice: No current price target; ~31x 2026 revenue reference. February-frame bull scenario implied $200+ (already exceeded). Stock described as 'strong fundamentals, extremely valuation-sensitive'

thesis: Coinbase is the monopolistic gateway to U.S. compliant crypto infrastructure, transitioning from a cyclical exchange to a diversified financial platform, but its fundamental dependence on crypto market sentiment is unchanged. The 2026H1 framework depends on trading, stablecoins, Base, custody, derivatives, and regulatory normalization together.
catalysts: BTC price trajectory (directly drives trading revenue)
Potential Kraken IPO reshaping competitive landscape
targetPrice: Bull scenario $350-450 (BTC $100K+, volume recovery); Bear scenario $80-120 (deep crypto bear). Analyst consensus $290-342

thesis: COPX offers leveraged exposure to a structural copper supercycle driven by electrification, AI infrastructure, and energy transition — but after a 105% one-year rally, the risk/reward balance demands careful timing. Copper has broken through February's most bullish forecast (~$13,531/tonne), yet COPX is essentially flat as P/E compressed from 30-35x to 17.74x.
catalysts: Mid-2026 Commerce Department Section 232 tariff review — largest near-term binary catalyst
Copper price trajectory (COMEX at ~$13,531/tonne, exceeding February bull case)
AI data center demand (475K tonnes in 2026)
targetPrice: If copper reaches $13,000-15,000/tonne by late 2026, COPX could see 30-50% additional upside. Bull case strengthened as copper exceeded February ceiling

thesis: Hims & Hers is the leading U.S. DTC telehealth platform with a powerful subscription engine, but the 2026H1 debate has shifted from an acute GLP-1 regulatory storm to a growth-quality audit: GLP-1/compound-drug durability, Novo/FDA risk, non-GLP-1 retention, and whether growth converts to cash.
catalysts: DOJ investigation status — whether the referral escalates to formal case
Novo Nordisk patent lawsuit — potential preliminary injunction
FDA statements on compounded GLP-1 policy
targetPrice: No explicit price target; event-driven binary outcome. P/S ~3.3x FY2025E revenue. Bear case: GLP-1 goes to zero + brand damage

thesis: IGV is the flagship U.S. software sector ETF offering concentrated exposure to North America's leading software companies, but the 2026 'Software-mageddon' — a panic selloff driven by AI disruption fears — placed it at a critical inflection point. Q1 2026 earnings showed most software companies' AI transition progress better than panic expectations, and IGV rebounded +17% from the $73.93 low.
catalysts: Q2 2026 earnings season (Jul-Aug) — next verification point for AI transition progress
AI feature ARPU contributions from MSFT Copilot, CRM Agentforce, etc.
Macro conditions affecting enterprise IT spending
Net Revenue Retention (NRR) stabilization across SaaS holdings
targetPrice: Analyst average target for IGV holdings ~$130 (~51% upside). Bull scenario $100-110 (+16-28%). Downside $65-70

thesis: Jinpan is a hidden champion in dry-type transformers, upgrading from a traditional manufacturer to a power-equipment solutions provider via AI data centers (AIDC) and solid-state transformers (SST). The 2026H1 debate has shifted from 'can the AI/SST story be discovered' to 'can a demanding valuation be supported by order conversion, cash flow, and receivable quality.'
catalysts: SST certification and first large orders (H2 2026 watchpoint)
H-share listing progress (EY engaged as auditor)
targetPrice: Neutral/watchlist — no current target-price call. Old SOTP midpoint ~RMB 31.6B. Current RMB 39.5B above that, embedding optimism. Downside to RMB 14-20B if execution falters

thesis: NeoVolta is a high-risk micro-cap riding the secular tailwind of U.S. residential energy storage adoption, with strategic moves toward domestic manufacturing (Georgia JV with LONGi) that could unlock IRA tax credit advantages. However, accelerating revenue contraction (Q1→Q2→Q3: $6.7M→$4.65M→$2.02M), heavy dilution (+65% shares), and persistent losses have raised the risk profile materially.
catalysts: Georgia factory ramp — slipped to end of August 2026
Infinite Grid Capital 1.1 GWh BESS LOI (~$200M, non-binding)
targetPrice: Needham price target $8 (+315% upside). Bull case $10-15/share; Base $5-7; Bear $1.50-3

thesis: Netflix is the undisputed global streaming leader with $45.2B revenue and 29.5% operating margin. The WBD acquisition is no longer Netflix's path after declining to raise its offer; the risk-reward now depends on organic growth, advertising monetization, content ROI, live/sports execution, and whether management can replace the lost inorganic IP catalyst.
catalysts: Post-WBD capital allocation (buybacks, original IP, licensing)
targetPrice: Analyst consensus target $116 (+43% upside from $71.82). Bull case: 2027 EPS $30+ at 35x P/E implies $100-105. Downside: $55-65

thesis: Oklo is one of the most imaginative plays in advanced nuclear — ~$1.2B cash, a 14.7GW+ pipeline, and marquee customers like Meta and Switch — but as a zero-revenue, pre-commercial company its ~$10B market cap is built entirely on a 5-10 year vision. The current framework must focus on regulation, first-reactor execution, fuel, customer conversion, and cash runway.
catalysts: Idaho first-reactor construction updates and DOE pilot-program 2026 criticality target
targetPrice: No current target-price call. Probability-weighted base case $50-80. February analyst consensus $103-122 (stale). Bull scenario $100-130; Bear $20-33

thesis: PayPal is a mature digital payments leader facing structural headwinds from intensifying competition and slowing branded checkout growth, trading at historically low valuations that reflect both legitimate concerns about execution and potential market overreaction. Branded checkout improved from ~1% (Q4) to ~2% (Q1 2026), and a new CEO (Lores) is executing a reorganization with ~$1.5B cost savings plan.
catalysts: Reorganization and ~$1.5B cost savings translating to EPS quality
Enrique Lores execution under new three-unit structure
targetPrice: Old $65-75 fair-value range stale — needs Q2 rebuild. FCF yield ~15-17% at current price. Forward P/E ~8x if 2026 adj. EPS in $5.0-5.5 range

thesis: Salesforce is the undisputed global CRM leader, now embedding AI deeply into its product matrix via Agentforce, transitioning from 'selling software' to 'selling AI workflows.' Stock has crashed from $193 to ~$151 (YTD -40%), Forward P/E from 17x to 11x. The debate has shifted from 'slowing growth at fair valuation' to 'deep value or value trap after the crash.' Business fundamentals (CRM dominance + margin expansion + $2.9B AI ARR) are intact.
catalysts: Agentforce ARR trajectory toward $2B+ (standalone; $800M in Q4 FY2026)
FY2027 execution and net retention stabilization
Agentforce + Data 360 combined ARR $2.9B+ (+200%+ YoY) scaling
$50B buyback authorization (80% of FCF for repurchases)
Non-GAAP operating margin expansion (34.1% → 34.3% FY27 guided)
targetPrice: Analyst consensus target $250 (+66% upside from $150.70). Bull case: Agentforce ARR to $2B+, 25x P/E implies $330+. FCF yield ~11.5%

thesis: SK hynix is the global HBM (High Bandwidth Memory) monopolist (53% share + NVIDIA lock-in), riding the AI-memory supercycle to record results — a 72% operating margin in Q1 2026. But the stock has risen ~3.5x since February and market cap has crossed $1.3T, so February's 'deeply undervalued at a single-digit forward P/E' thesis has largely played out; the debate shifts from 'is it cheap' to 'how durable is the peak.'
catalysts: Q2 2026 — whether peak margins (72%) hold and ASPs top out
HBM4 mass-production progress and Samsung share shifts (H2 2026)
HBM pricing pressure as all three expand HBM4 capacity
2027 — whether the memory cycle peaks; HBM4E mass production
targetPrice: No current target-price call. February's 'deep value' window closed (TTM P/E now ~28x vs 14.2x old). Forward P/E ~13-16x on peak-cycle earnings. Described as 'high-quality franchise at a full-ish price, near a cyclical peak'

thesis: Spotify has successfully transformed from a chronically unprofitable music streaming platform into a profitable 'global audio technology platform.' Q1 2026 operating margin reached 15.77%, P/E compressed from ~53-66x to ~30x as earnings caught up, but current valuation still requires digesting high growth expectations even after a significant pullback.
catalysts: 2026 pricing cycle (February US price hike of $1/month, ~$500M annualized incremental revenue)
AI-driven user engagement (AI playlists, Smart Shuffle Plus)
Audiobook integration as third content pillar
Video podcast growth and Netflix content partnership
targetPrice: DCF base ~$500 (~13% upside from $442). Analyst consensus ~$595 (~34% upside). Bull case: $600-700 if 15%+ operating margin with 15% revenue growth. Downside: $300-350

thesis: Tempus AI is precision medicine's 'data flywheel' company — collecting data through diagnostic testing, analyzing it with AI, and licensing it to pharma. Revenue keeps compounding fast (Q1 2026 +36.1% organic), but GAAP losses are widening (-$125.9M in Q1), the CEO is a consistent seller ($127M+ cumulative), and short interest exceeds 20%. A high-growth, high-risk proposition.
catalysts: MRD Medicare reimbursement (2026 — volume already ~6,500 in Q1, +500% YoY)
xT CDx FDA approval-pathway migration (raises reimbursement price)
Merck pharma collaboration (Q1 2026, validates data-licensing demand)
Q2 2026 — whether +36% pace holds and loss curve bends
targetPrice: No explicit price target stated. P/S(TTM) ~7x; EV/2026E EBITDA ~150x. Described as 'valuation not cheap for a still-unprofitable company with widening GAAP losses'
